questions.question: How did you hear about us?
insights: Esthetician referrals drive the highest AOV ($118) despite being only 17% of responses — professional channel punches above its weight.
TikTok drives volume (10%) but lowest AOV ($54) — primarily attracting deal-seekers.
Word-of-mouth (21%) has the second highest AOV ($105) — loyal customers are the best acquisition channel.
Meta Ads are the #1 reported channel but AOV trails referrals by 35% — targeting may be too broad.

questions.question: What was the main reason you decided to purchase today?

responses.answer: Trying to solve a specific skin concern
Saw a review or recommendation online

deal-seeker: Saw a review or recommendation online
skin-concern-solver: Trying to solve a specific skin concern

insights: 32% are replenishment purchases — strong retention signal. CRM should optimize restock reminders.
20% are promotion-driven — confirms deal-seeker persona is real and sizable.
Professional recommendations drive 18% of purchases — this channel needs more investment.
Only 9% cite online reviews — suggests reviews are a validator, not a primary driver.

questions.question: What almost stopped you from buying today?

responses.answer: Nothing, it was an easy decision
Wasn't sure which product to choose
Wanted to compare with other brands first
Wasn't sure it would work for my skin

ingredient-conscious: Nothing, it was an easy decision
Wanted to compare with other brands first
routine-simplifier: Wasn't sure which product to choose
Nothing, it was an easy decision
skin-concern-solver: Wasn't sure it would work for my skin
Wasn't sure which product to choose
brand-loyalist: Nothing, it was an easy decision

insights: Price is the #1 blocker (31%) — reinforces need for value messaging and loyalty program.
14% weren't sure which product to choose — the product selection/quiz experience is a CRO priority.
Shipping concerns block 12% — lowering free shipping threshold would directly impact conversion.
24% said 'nothing' — these are high-intent buyers, likely brand loyalists and restock customers.

questions.question: In your mind, what is the product's biggest benefit to you?

responses.answer: Visible results / my skin looks better

insights: 'Visible results' is the #1 perceived benefit (34%) — this should be the hero message across all channels.
'Professional-grade quality' (21%) validates the professional trust theme — lean into esthetician credibility.
'Clean ingredients' (16%) maps directly to the ingredient-conscious persona.
Only 6% cite 'value' — the brand is NOT perceived as a value play. Premium positioning is correct.

questions.question: How are you planning to use the product you purchased?

responses.answer: Trying it out for the first time

insights: 44% use for daily routine — CRM should optimize for replenishment timing.
25% treating a specific issue — post-purchase follow-up should ask about results at 30/60/90 days.
8% are gifts — seasonal gift campaigns and gift set merchandising are an opportunity.
7% are first-time triers — critical to convert these to repeat buyers with strong onboarding flow.

questions.question: What types of communications would you prefer to receive?
insights: 33% want new product launches — CRM should prioritize launch announcements to this segment.
26% prefer education — maps to ingredient-conscious persona; fuel the education email series.
21% want sales/promos — confirms deal-seeker segment size; use for promotional flow targeting.
Self-segmentation via this question can directly feed Klaviyo profile properties for personalization.
